title: Pecteilis vatia
intro: Pecteilis philippinensis, commonly known as Pecteilis vatia, is a species of orchid native to China and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, respiratory issues, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Pecteilis vatia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Pecteilis vatia is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and is also used in combination with other herbs to make tonics and infusions for promoting vitality and relieving stress.
taxonomy: Pecteilis vatia is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This species is native to East Asia and is known for its unique flowers and growth habits. Pecteilis vatia is often found growing in woodland and rocky areas, where it can thrive in partial shade. Its classification as a member of the Orchidaceae family places it among some 30,000 other species of orchids worldwide. Further classification includes its subfamily, Orchidoideae, and tribe, Cranichideae.
